_comment: The Config·JSON value for the self-training loop. labeled_seed / unlabeled_pool / test_set are s3-ref values (typed s3-ref so they wire into the Train Model channel ports + pseudo_label's pool dir — a json value cannot wire into an s3-ref port). target_acc / conf_threshold / max_rounds are scalar loop knobs. On the canvas: Config·JSON → flow.loop[ train → pseudo_label → select_confident → merge ] → Notify. The connect-stack SageMaker exec role reads s3://try-conduit-app/examples/* directly; data elsewhere must live under a conduit-* bucket (or widen the exec role + redeploy the connect stack).

_loop_config._comment: How this wires into the flow.loop node on the canvas. The loop body is train → pseudo_label → select_confident → merge; merge is the body's sink. Loop VARS seed body nodes by name and update from merge's outputs each round: `trainingData` seeds train's `trainingData` channel port (channel `train`) and is updatedBy merge's `trainingDataNext`; `pool` seeds pseudo_label's `pool` and is updatedBy merge's `poolNext`; `metrics` is stop-only (no body input) and is updatedBy merge's `metrics`. `validationData` is a SHARED (non-var) input wired from test_set into train's `validationData` channel port (channel `validation`) — constant across rounds. The model handoff (train.model → pseudo_label.model) threads as JSON (the model-artifact ref), not a staged file. The loop halts when held-out accuracy clears target_acc OR maxRounds is hit. (NOTE: registering the FINAL model into the Model Registry after the loop ends is a FOLLOW-ON — not in this example.)
